title: Theater Arts Annual Program Review 2018-19 AY (Part 1)
text:
College of the Siskiyous Annual Program Review- Theatre Arts AY 2018-19
1. Describe annual progress / changes / events in the program
This year the Theatre program has focused on 3 key areas, recruitment, retention, and
completion, all of which are aligned with the overall goals of the institution. The following are a
brief synopis of the work being done in these areas;
Recruitment
For the past several years the Theatre program has focused on reaching out to K-12
students in the Siskiyous county region. We have done this in a variety of way in the hopes of
providing impactful experiences for area youths in the performing arts, they are as follows;
High School Workshops
Etna High School
The Theater program attended rehearsals and provided training for actors
involved in their Fall 2018 production
Scott Valley Junior High School
The Theater program conducted teacher training with lighting equipment
for their Fall 2018 production
Student Matinees
Fall 2018 “Little Shop of Horrors”
Over 700 Middle and High Schools attended our student matinees for this
production. Our student matinees include a talk back with the cast and
crew as well a backstage tour.
A student matinee for area high schools is planned for our Spring 2019
production of “Antigone”
Summer Camps
Summer 2018 Musical Theater Intensive
We have changed format from our previous camp which focused on
production to a more process oriented experience.
School Tours
Spring 2019 High School Tour (Proposed)
We have had success in the past attracting students by bringing our
productions to area schools, however currently we lack the monetary
resources to implement this much needed recruitment strategy.
Please see CQIP 1

-- 1 of 4 --

title: Theater Arts Annual Program Review 2018-19 AY (Part 2)
text:
College of the Siskiyous Annual Program Review- Theatre Arts AY 2018-19
Retention
With our recent full-time instructional support positions in place, we have intentionally
increased our number of productions to both meet the needs of students and to provide more
opportunities for our community to engage with the Theater program, and the Institution in
general. The Theater program continues to collaborate with the Music program in an effort to
foster a multi-disciplinary approach to our subject matter which helps us better serve the diverse
needs of our students. Below is a list of our productions for this year;
Summer 2018 Walking with Walt (Community production)
Fall 2018 Little Shop of Horrors (Collegiate level Musical Theater production)
The Musical Theater Degree continues to be our most popular award in
the performing arts area. A performing arts accompanist is needed to
support the needs of students so they may complete their degrees.
Please see CQIP 2
Fall 2018 Electro-magnetic Soul Vehicle Workshop (Community production)
Winter 2019 Electro-Magnetic Soul Vehicle (Community production)
Spring 2019 Antigone (Collegiate level Dramatic production)
Completion (Awards, Degrees and Certificates)
Kennedy Center American College Theater Festival (KCACTF)
A few years ago we joined the American College Theater Festival and it has paid real
dividends for us. Many of our students have had the opportunity to engage with 4 year
schools and get a real world experience which has in turn helped to guide their future
college plans. We feel that this will encourage students to finish their degrees.
Update and Creation of Theater Degrees
The Theater program continues to strive for currency so that we may better serve our
students with their plans for future education or a profession in the performing arts, plans
to update with it’s degree and certificates are in place, they are listed below;
Update Musical Theater Degree
Create Technical Theater Degree
Update Technical Theater Certificate

-- 2 of 4 --

title: Theater Arts Annual Program Review 2018-19 AY (Part 3)
text:
College of the Siskiyous Annual Program Review- Theatre Arts AY 2018-19
2. Describe Progress Assessing SLOs / PLOs
This spring the theater program will begin Course Mapping, all assessments in all classes
will be mapped to course SLOs, and then all course SLOs will be mapped to PLOs. One step we
will take to is to create a Canvas shell for all courses so that all assessments will automatically be
linked to course SLOs. Another step will to ensure that all courses have rubrics for each
assessment. PLOs should also be updated, below is an example for the Interstate Passport
Creative Expression Area we are considering adopting;
Identify, explain and/or demonstrate relationships among societal, cultural, and historical
contexts.
Demonstrate knowledge of and empathy for the diversity of values, beliefs, ideas, and
practices embodied in the human experience.
Use appropriate methods and tools to analyze, interpret and critique creative processes,
works, and/or presentations.
Engage in a creative process through experimentation, reflection, tolerance for failure,
and revision.
Employ fundamental discipline-specific principles, terminology, skills, technology, and
methods.
3. Support or Obstacles
Insufficient Scenic and Properies Storage
We continue to suffer from a lack of sufficient storage for our scenery and props. As we
increase our number of productions (and our student count continues to increase) so does
our need for appropriate storage for our inventory.
Please see CQIP 3
Repair of Lighting Postion
5 years ago, our fly system was inspected and the the front of house lighting position in
Kenneth Ford Theater was found to be out of compliance so we have been unable to use
it since. This is an important component of the lighting system in the theater and we have
been unable to adequately light the stage since the inspection.
Please see CQIP 4
Repair Budget Funded
For the current academic year the Theater program has not been given a budget for
crucially needed repairs to equipment and for ongoing expenses such as replacement of

-- 3 of 4 --

College of the Siskiyous Annual Program Review- Theatre Arts AY 2018-19
theater related inventory, such as; dust collection for the scene shop, replacement of black
masking drapery, upgrading to new striplights.
Please see CQIP 5
Full time Faculty Dance/Theater
COS has had the good fortune of being able to hire a qualified part-time instructor and
choreographer for the past few semesters. Within that time we have demonstrated that the
theater program operates the most effectively and efficiently when there are two faculty
members present. Hiring an additional Full time Theater instructor will provide more
stability with an aready known outcome of success.
Please see CQIP 6
4. Describe program budgetary needs or implications
Below is a list of the Theater Program budgetary needs, identified other areas of this program
review;
CQIP 1 High School Tour
CQIP 2 Accompanist
CQIP 3 Storage for Props and Scenery
CQIP 4 Lighting Position Repair
CQIP 5 Repair Budget
CQIP 6 Full Time Theater/Dance Instructor
